award_title: Machine Learning for Resilient Drone Swarm Coordination
abstract: This Phase II effort develops machine learning models that let drone swarms keep coordinating when communications are degraded or denied. The team trains reinforcement learning policies in simulation and transitions them to flight hardware for maritime patrol missions.

award_title: Computer Vision for Onboard ISR Targeting
abstract: The project builds a computer vision pipeline that runs on size-, weight-, and power-constrained airborne sensors to detect and classify ground objects in real time, reducing operator workload during intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance flights.

award_title: Neural Network Acceleration for Electronic Warfare
abstract: This award funds a low-latency neural network accelerator that classifies radar and communications signals at the tactical edge. The deep neural network is quantized to run on a rugged embedded processor.

award_title: Lightweight Composite Structures for Hypersonic Vehicles
abstract: The effort develops a ceramic-matrix composite that withstands sustained high-temperature loads on hypersonic airframes. Coupon testing will characterize oxidation resistance and structural margin.

award_title: Containerized Cold-Chain Resupply for Expeditionary Bases
abstract: This project designs a modular refrigerated container that keeps medical supplies within temperature range during expeditionary resupply. The design emphasizes rapid setup and low fuel consumption.
The platform fuses open-source data to forecast geopolitical risk. It produces early indications and warning for analysts tracking emerging threats.
